title: HotfixResult
description: Output of `bin/hotfix.sh`. Sets up the branch topology for a patch release against a previously tagged version: ensures a `release/<major>.<minor>` branch exists from the source tag, then creates a `hotfix/<slug>` branch off it. The user commits their fix there; `/nyann:release --version <patch>` does the rest.

target.description: Repo path the hotfix flow ran against.

source_tag.description: Tag the hotfix lineage starts from (the `--from` argument).

release_branch.description: Long-lived branch the patch release will be tagged from. Defaults to `release/<major>.<minor>` derived from the source tag; overridable via `--release-branch`.

hotfix_branch.description: Short-lived branch the user commits the fix on. Always `hotfix/<slug>`.

release_branch_created.description: True when the release branch didn't already exist locally and was created from the source tag in this run. False when it already existed (idempotent).

hotfix_branch_created.description: True when the hotfix branch was created in this run. Always true when this script succeeds — it refuses if the branch already exists (no silent overwrite).

checked_out.description: True when --checkout was passed AND the script switched to the hotfix branch successfully. Absent when --checkout wasn't requested.

next_steps.description: Concrete shell snippets the user runs next. Includes: switch to the hotfix branch (if --checkout wasn't passed), commit the fix, run `/nyann:release --version <patch> --base <release_branch>` to tag and push.
